notes: All entities are fictional; verification must run against the source files in each fixture directory only. seeded_error null = clean claim. A 'finding' for scoring = any claim whose label is not Verified. Every fixture contains at least one DISTRACTOR source that superficially supports a false claim; 'notes' on a claim explains why direct lookup fails and what method catches it. Verified claims marked trap=true in notes look wrong on a shallow read — they test false alarms.

claims.claim: Meridian Labs launched Atlas Edge on March 3, 2026.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md: release dated March 3, 2026 announcing GA of Atlas Edge.

claims.claim: Quarterly revenue hit $8 million.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-earnings-letter.md: 'annualized revenue run rate crossed $8 million during the quarter' — the $8M is an annualized run rate, not quarterly revenue (quarterly would be ~$2M).
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: '$8 million', 'revenue', and 'Q1' all appear together in the letter, so a keyword match confirms the claim. Catching it requires reading the figure's unit — run rate vs quarterly — not just its value.

claims.claim: CEO Dana Whitfield said: 'Atlas Edge cuts inference cost in half for every customer.'
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md (primary): actual quote is 'cuts inference cost by a third for our median customer'. source-techwire-coverage.md prints the altered version.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the quoted sentence appears verbatim in the TechWire piece, so a search 'verifies' it. Source-tier climbing catches it: the primary press release the article covers has different words, and the secondary outlet is the one that distorted them.

claims.claim: Atlas Edge now runs in 7,500 active deployments.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-q1-update.md: 7,500 was the January 2026 private-beta figure; May 2026 figure is 12,000. source-techwire-coverage.md repeats 7,500 as if current at launch.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: 7,500 appears in two sources, which looks like corroboration. Lateral reading plus staleness checking catch it: both mentions trace to the same beta-era figure, and the newest source from the same origin supersedes it.

claims.claim: Atlas Edge supports fully on-prem deployment.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md: 'ships with support for fully on-prem deployment'.

claims.claim: According to the company's May customer update, the team has grown to 60 people.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-q1-update.md (the cited May update) says nothing about headcount; source-earnings-letter.md settles it: headcount is 52.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails if the checker stops at 'cited source is silent' and labels Missing-source. The method requires sweeping the other provided sources: a different source settles the figure, and it contradicts the claim.

claims.claim: Meridian raised a $30 million Series B.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions funding.

claims.claim: Atlas Edge is the fastest edge runtime on the market.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-earnings-letter.md engages the topic: third-party benchmarks underway, results expected Q3; the company itself makes no comparative claims. Engaged but unsettled — not Missing-source.

claims.claim: Error rates among short sleepers nearly doubled on attention tasks.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md (primary): 22% higher error rates, relative. source-newsdesk-summary.md says 'nearly doubled' — the distortion the article copied.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the newsdesk summary supports 'nearly doubled' word for word. Tier climbing catches it: the preprint abstract is the origin and says 22%.

claims.claim: Raman said 'You'd conclude shift work is safe — the raw error rates look modest', playing down the findings.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-author-interview.md: exact words, right speaker — but the full sentence is a hypothetical she immediately refutes ('But once we controlled... the deficit was unmistakable'). The article deploys it to mean the opposite.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: words and speaker both check out verbatim. Only the third quote axis — original context — catches it: the surrounding transcript reverses the meaning the article gives the quote.

claims.claim: The study tracked 480 shift workers over twelve weeks using wrist actigraphy and nightly task batteries.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: methods match exactly.

claims.claim: Sleeping under six hours causes a 22% rise in attention errors.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: 'was associated with' — an observational association. source-institute-statement.md: 'causal claims would be premature'. The paraphrase upgrades association to causation.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the 22% figure and both nouns match the abstract. The catch is reading what the paraphrase changed — 'associated with' became 'causes' — which a number-matching pass never sees.

claims.claim: After two weeks of longer sleep, participants' error rates returned to baseline.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: recovery arm underway, results expected late 2026; source-institute-statement.md: 'not yet analyzed'; source-author-interview.md: 'still in the field'. Sources engage; none settles.

claims.claim: More than 90% of participants completed the full protocol.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: Genuine conflict: source-institute-statement.md says completion 'ran above 90%'; source-study-abstract.md (primary) says 412 of 480 completed = 85.8%. Report both; the primary is higher-tier.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the institute statement confirms the claim outright. Triangulation catches it: the primary abstract's own count contradicts the press office, and conflict between credible sources adjudicates to Disputed, not Verified.

claims.claim: The study cost $2 million and is the institute's largest sleep project to date.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions cost. ('Largest cohort in our history' in the statement does not address cost or project ranking by size of budget.)

claims.claim: Short-sleep participants averaged 5.2 hours of sleep on work nights.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: exact figure. trap=true: the distractor newsdesk says 'under five hours' — a checker leaning on the secondary source false-alarms here; the primary confirms 5.2.

claims.claim: The council approved a $4.6 million parks and recreation budget at its April 14 meeting.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-mayor-newsletter.md (pre-vote) touts the proposed $4.6M; source-council-minutes.md: a floor amendment cut the total to $4.2M before adoption; source-budget-document.md confirms $4.2M adopted.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: '$4.6 million parks and recreation budget' matches the newsletter. The catch is timeline reading: the newsletter predates the vote, and the primary record (minutes, adopted budget) supersedes it.

claims.claim: The vote was unanimous.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-mayor-newsletter.md predicted unanimous support; source-council-minutes.md: actual vote 6–1 (Castellanos opposed). Prediction superseded by the primary record.

claims.claim: Ruiz drew a hard line: 'I will not vote for a budget that cuts pool hours' (framed as opposition).
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-council-minutes.md: exact words, right speaker — but the next sentence is 'Fortunately, this one doesn't — it restores them. It has my vote.' Ruiz supported the budget; the article frames the quote as dissent.

claims.claim: Trail maintenance funding rises 20% over last year.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: Triangulation required: source-budget-document.md FY27 trail maintenance $600,000; source-staff-memo.md FY26 actual $500,000; 500k to 600k = +20%. No single source states the percentage.

claims.claim: The city budgeted $150,000 a quarter for aquatics supplies.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-budget-document.md: aquatics supplies $150,000 annual. The amount matches; the timeframe is quadrupled.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: '$150,000' and 'aquatics supplies' match the budget line exactly. Catching it requires checking the period attached to the figure — annual, not quarterly.

claims.claim: The budget puts Linden on track to clear its parks maintenance backlog within two years.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-staff-memo.md engages: backlog is $3.1M and elimination 'depends on sustained funding through FY29', which has not been proposed. Engaged but unsettled.

claims.claim: Linden's park system is the largest in the county.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source addresses county-wide park-system rankings.

claims.claim: Daily ridership stands at 14,000.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-press-kit-2025.md: 14,000 was the November 2025 opening-week figure; source-agency-update.md: 18,400 average weekday in April 2026.

claims.claim: The extension's final cost came in at $310 million.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: Genuine conflict: source-agency-update.md says the project 'remains within its $310 million budget'; source-oversight-report.md puts total cost exposure at $338M including $28M in unsettled contractor claims and calls the agency's framing misleading. Also: certified costs not due until Q4 2026, so 'final' overstates either source.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the agency's own update confirms $310M. Triangulation catches it: an independent, current, credible source contradicts the agency, and contradiction beats support — the label is Disputed with both sources shown.

claims.claim: According to the agency's April update, trains run every six minutes at peak.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-agency-update.md (the cited source) says nothing about headways; source-press-kit-2025.md settles it: ten minutes at peak. The cited source is silent and a different source contradicts the figure.

claims.claim: The project is run by director Lena Cho, a 20-year veteran of the agency.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-agency-update.md: Cho leads the program and 'has served the agency for 20 years'.

claims.claim: Mayor Otis predicted the line 'would never carry more than ten thousand riders a day'.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-mayor-remarks.md: Otis spoke those exact words — quoting critics in order to refute them ('Critics said... They were wrong'). The article reverses speaker stance: he celebrated the ridership, not doubted it.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the words appear in the mayor's transcript, from the mayor's mouth. Context verification catches it: the sentence frame ('Critics said... They were wrong') flips who held the prediction.

claims.claim: The line has spurred $200 million in new development along the corridor.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-oversight-report.md engages: an economic-impact study is commissioned, findings due 2027, and the board cautions against citing development figures before then. Engaged but unsettled.

claims.claim: Crestline is the region's first fully automated rail line.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source addresses automation.

claims.claim: Ridership has grown roughly 30% since opening.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: Triangulation required: source-press-kit-2025.md opening figure 14,000; source-agency-update.md April figure 18,400; +31.4% ≈ 'roughly 30%'. trap=true: no single source states a growth rate, and the same article misuses 14,000 as current — the growth claim is still right.

claims.claim: Ridgeline will spend $5 million a year on coastal wetlands restoration.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-grant-announcement.md (primary): $5 million in total over four years. source-coastal-times-article.md repeats the '$5 million a year' misreading.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the Coastal Times sentence matches the claim almost word for word. Tier climbing catches it: the foundation's own announcement says total over four years — the secondary outlet quadrupled the program.

claims.claim: Individual grants max out at $250,000 a year per recipient.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: Triangulation required: source-grant-announcement.md caps awards at $1M per recipient over the program; source-program-faq.md disburses over four years in equal annual installments; $1M / 4 = $250,000 per year. No single source states the annual figure.

claims.claim: Marsh said wetlands are a cheaper replacement for seawalls.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-grant-announcement.md: Marsh said wetlands 'complement hard infrastructure — they don't replace it'. The paraphrase asserts the replacement she explicitly denied. source-coastal-times-article.md's 'wetlands beat seawalls' gloss supports the false reading.

claims.claim: The program spans three states.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-grant-announcement.md (January) and source-coastal-times-article.md (February) both say three states; source-program-faq.md (updated May 2026): now four states, Gulfport added in April. Two older sources agree and are both superseded.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: two of three sources confirm 'three states', which reads as solid corroboration. Staleness checking catches it: the newest same-origin source supersedes both.

claims.claim: Nine universities will receive funding under the program.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-program-faq.md engages: no recipients confirmed, list finalized July 2026, and nonprofits are also eligible. Engaged but unsettled.

claims.claim: Ridgeline has a $2 billion endowment.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions the endowment.

claims.claim: Disbursement is gated by annual progress reviews.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-program-faq.md: equal annual installments, each gated by an annual progress review.

claims.claim: Volt Gaming announced its acquisition of Pixel Forge on May 20, 2026.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: dated May 20, 2026.

claims.claim: The combined organization fields 11 competitive teams across six titles.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: 11 across six was the at-closing figure; source-roster-page.md (June 8, 2026): 13 teams across seven titles. source-fan-wiki.md repeats the stale 11.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: 11/six appears in two sources. The wiki copied the release; the official roster page is newer and same-origin. Lateral reading collapses the two matches into one stale origin.

claims.claim: The deal is valued at $90 million.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: Only source-fan-wiki.md carries the figure — 'reportedly', citation needed, community-edited. source-merger-release.md: 'Financial terms were not disclosed'; source-investor-faq.md: 'We do not comment on deal valuation'. Sources engage; the only support is a low-tier uncorroborated source, so nothing settles it. Not Missing-source.
claims.notes: Direct lookup fails: the wiki states $90 million plainly, so a shallow pass marks Verified. Tier judgment catches it: an anonymous community edit with 'citation needed' cannot carry a claim both primary sources decline to confirm.

claims.claim: Per the merger release, the combined academy program fields 200 players.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md (the cited source) says nothing about academy headcount; source-investor-faq.md settles it: 80 players under contract.

claims.claim: Chen admitted that 'Pixel Forge disappears into Volt'.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: the words are Chen's, but they are the question she refutes — 'People keep asking whether Pixel Forge disappears into Volt. The honest answer is no'. The article presents her denial as an admission.

claims.claim: Volt contributes more teams to the merger than Pixel Forge.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: Triangulation within the release: combined 11 teams at closing, Pixel Forge brings four, so Volt contributes seven. No sentence states the comparison directly.

claims.claim: Volt, founded in 2018, grew from a single fighting-game roster.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: 'founded in 2018 as a single fighting-game roster'. trap=true: source-fan-wiki.md says 2017 — a checker that weighs the low-tier wiki against the primary release false-alarms here.

claims.claim: Volt's flagship team has won back-to-back league titles.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source addresses team championships.
